apiVersion.beta: v1beta1 (path prefix /api/v1beta1/) for the Backtesting/historical endpoint family, explicitly flagged 'Beta - subject to breaking changes'
apiVersion.versioningPolicy: Path-based versioning. Legacy /api/beta/* endpoints were restructured to /api/v1/* in the '29-08-2025: Major API Restructuring' changelog (snake_case fields, no nested parameters wrapper, standardized data+pagination responses). No formal written deprecation timeline policy found; instead the docs mark parameters as deprecated in-schema (e.g. token-screener 'date' deprecated in favor of 'timeframe', 'only_smart_money' deprecated in favor of 'trader_type', prediction-market 'sort_by' deprecated in favor of 'order_by') and announce behavior changes in the API Changelog (e.g. 31-03-2026 entry: premium_labels default flips from true to false on April 1).

authentication.mechanism: API key in an HTTP request header
authentication.headerName: apikey (authentication page shows lowercase 'apikey' and advises 'Ensure the header name is apikey (lowercase)'; the embedded OpenAPI securityScheme and the 'Make Your First API Call' page write it as 'apiKey' - HTTP header names are case-insensitive)
authentication.type: OpenAPI securityScheme: type apiKey, in header, name apiKey
authentication.obtaining: Generate in the Nansen Dashboard (app.nansen.ai, API tab -> Create API Key) or via the Agent Setup flow at https://app.nansen.ai/auth/agent-setup

alternativeKeylessAccess.x402: Pay-per-call in USDC on Base or Solana. Request without a key returns 402 Payment Required with payment instructions; client pays and retries with a payment receipt. Facilitators: Base = Coinbase CDP, Solana = Payai. If a valid API key is included it takes precedence and no x402 payment is required.
alternativeKeylessAccess.mppTempo: Machine Payments Protocol on Tempo mainnet (chainId 4217). Send 'Authorization: Payment', receive 402 with a WWW-Authenticate: Payment challenge (~30s expiry), sign with a funded Tempo wallet, retry; success returns a Payment-Receipt header. Adds ~one Tempo block-time latency per call; no session reuse.
alternativeKeylessAccess.exclusions: /api/v1/labels/* endpoints are excluded from pay-per-call (Pro subscription only, proprietary entity data); the x402 page also lists /api/v1/portfolio/defi-holdings under 'Excluded Endpoints'. Discovery endpoint: GET https://api.nansen.ai/.well-known/x402

authentication.mcpAuth: Nansen MCP server at https://mcp.nansen.ai/ra/mcp authenticates with a NANSEN-API-KEY header (JSON-RPC 2.0 over Streamable HTTP/SSE; requires 'Accept: application/json, text/event-stream')

baseUrls.purpose: Main REST API host; production endpoints under /api/v1/, backtesting beta endpoints under /api/v1beta1/. No plan-specific hosts are documented - Free and Pro use the same host.
Official Nansen MCP server (24 tools) for AI-agent access; same credits/API-key system as REST.
Permissionless Nansen Points Rewards API (GET, no authentication required; separate from the main API).

endpoints.name: Smart Money Netflow
optionalParams: filters.include_smart_money_labels / exclude_smart_money_labels (enum: Fund, Smart Trader, 30D/90D/180D Smart Trader, Smart HL Perps Trader)
responseTopLevelFields: data[] {token_address, token_symbol, net_flow_1h_usd, net_flow_24h_usd, net_flow_7d_usd, net_flow_30d_usd, chain, token_sectors, trader_count, token_age_days, market_cap_usd}
pagination {page, per_page, is_last_page}
endpoints.freshness: Aggregated over rolling 1h/24h/7d/30d windows; includes DEX trades and CEX transfers. Not explicitly latency-quantified.
endpoints.redistribution: Restricted (approval + significant modification) per Data Redistribution Guidelines (listed as smart-money/inflows, the former name of this endpoint)

endpoints.name: Smart Money Holdings
optionalParams: filters {include/exclude_smart_money_labels, include_stablecoins (default false), include_native_tokens (default false), value_usd, balance_24h_percent_change, holders_count, share_of_holdings_percent, token_age_days, market_cap_usd, token_address, token_symbol, token_sectors}
responseTopLevelFields: data[] {chain, token_address, token_symbol, token_sectors, value_usd, balance_24h_percent_change, holders_count, share_of_holdings_percent, token_age_days, market_cap_usd}
pagination
endpoints.freshness: Docs state '24-hour balance change tracking updated in realtime'; aggregated balances, not per-wallet.
endpoints.redistribution: PROHIBITED - internal use only per Data Redistribution Guidelines

endpoints.name: Smart Money Historical Holdings
endpoints.status: beta ('data may be subject to changes, corrections, or improvements')
requiredParams: date range (per endpoint description: date range filtering, max 4 years lookback)
optionalParams: same filtering options as current holdings endpoint
responseTopLevelFields: daily snapshot records with point-in-time value_usd (median price of that day), market_cap_usd (historical snapshot), balance_24h_percent_change (day-over-day with each day's own price)
pagination
endpoints.freshness: Daily end-of-day UTC snapshots. Processing begins 05:00 UTC; data typically available by 07:00 UTC; current (incomplete) day excluded. 4-year rolling window, earliest date advances daily. No look-ahead bias by design.
endpoints.redistribution: not explicitly listed in redistribution table

endpoints.name: Smart Money DEX Trades
responseTopLevelFields: data[] (transaction-level trades)
pagination
endpoints.freshness: 'Real-time DEX trading activity ... over the last 24 hours' - rolling 24h window only.

endpoints.name: Profiler - Address Current Balance
requiredParams: chain (use 'all' for cross-chain; enum incl. all, arbitrum, avalanche, base, bitcoin, bnb, ethereum, hyperevm, injective, iotaevm, linea, mantle, mantra, monad, near, optimism, plasma, polygon, robinhood, ronin, scroll, sei, solana, sonic, starknet, sui, ton, tron)
optionalParams: address OR entity_name (address field in response empty when entity_name used)
filters {value_usd, price_usd, token_amount, token_symbol, token_address, token_name}
responseTopLevelFields: data[] ProfilerBalance {chain, address, token_address, token_symbol, token_name, token_amount (decimal-adjusted number; no raw base-unit amount and no token decimals exposed), price_usd, value_usd}
pagination
endpoints.freshness: Described as 'real-time token balance information'.
endpoints.redistribution: Allowed, no attribution required (listed as profiler/address/balances)

endpoints.name: Profiler - Address Transactions
optionalParams: pagination (max 100 records/page per schema; changelog notes max reduced from 100 to 20 in v1 migration)
responseTopLevelFields: data[] transactions incl. token transfers, native movements, contract interactions
pagination
endpoints.freshness: Recent blockchain transactions; latency not quantified per endpoint (FAQ: 'often within seconds, depending on the chain').

endpoints.name: Profiler - Address Counterparties
responseTopLevelFields: data[] counterparties with volume, frequency, timing, net flows
pagination
endpoints.freshness: Not documented per endpoint

endpoints.name: Profiler - Address Labels / Premium Labels
requiredParams: chain ('all' searches all chains compatible with the address format)
responseTopLevelFields: labels: ENS domains, behavioral, DeFi, CEX labels on /labels; premium adds smart money, alpha trader, public figure tags on /premium-labels
endpoints.freshness: Not documented

endpoints.name: Profiler - Address PnL Summary
requiredParams: address (per docs pattern; exact schema not extracted)
responseTopLevelFields: aggregate PnL statistics: realized PnL, win rate, top 5 profitable tokens
endpoints.freshness: Not documented

endpoints.name: Token Screener
requiredParams: chains (1-5 items; 27-chain enum incl. ethereum, solana, base, bnb, arbitrum, ton, tron, sui, starknet, near, monad, hyperevm...)
timeframe (5m|10m|1h|6h|24h|7d|30d) - required unless deprecated 'date' is used
optionalParams: filters {token_address, token_symbol, trader_type (all|sm|whale|public_figure|trending|consistent_perps_winner|high_winrate_hl_perps_trader|predicted_winner), only_smart_money (deprecated), sectors, exclude_sectors, token_age_days, market_cap_usd, liquidity, price_usd, price_change, fdv, fdv_mc_ratio, nof_buyers/sellers/traders/buys/sells, buy_volume, sell_volume, volume, netflow, inflow_fdv_ratio, outflow_fdv_ratio, include_stablecoins (default true), include/exclude_smart_money_labels}
responseTopLevelFields: data[] {chain, token_address, token_symbol, token_age_days/hours, token_deployment_date, market_cap_usd, liquidity, price_usd, price_change, fdv, fdv_mc_ratio, nof_* trader counts (variant-dependent), buy_volume, sell_volume, volume, netflow, inflow/outflow_fdv_ratio}
pagination
endpoints.freshness: Real-time analytics with strict retention: minute granularity ~last 130 minutes (schema: 2-hour retention), hourly ~last 50 hours (schema: 2-day), daily ~last 50 days (schema: 2-month). to_date must be within 5 minutes of current time; historical snapshots NOT supported (use token-screener/historical for that).

endpoints.name: TGM - Token Information
responseTopLevelFields: basic details (name, symbol, contract, logo), deployment date, social links, market cap, FDV, spot metrics (volume, buys/sells, unique traders, liquidity, holders); includes btc_drop_reflexivity quant-signal object {signal, avg_signal, volatility, signal_percentile, score, drops_in_window, is_stablecoin}
endpoints.freshness: Per requested date range/timeframe; btc_drop_reflexivity updates only when new BTC drop events occur (returns null with <4 qualifying drops in last 365 days)

endpoints.name: TGM - Holders
optionalParams: label_type (e.g. all_holders, smart_money, whale, exchange; must match labels filter)
premium_labels (currently defaults true; default flips to false per changelog - Free plan gets 403 when true)
responseTopLevelFields: data[] top holders with balances, historical activity, recent balance changes; sortable by balance_usd, balance_change_24h_usd, first/last_transfer_timestamp
pagination
endpoints.freshness: Current state. Native-token all_holders queries use an optimized model with limited ordering (token_amount only) and limited filters.

endpoints.name: TGM - Flow Intelligence
responseTopLevelFields: inflows/outflows/netflows per holder segment (Exchanges, Smart Money, Public Figures, Whales, Top PnL Traders, Fresh Wallets) with time-based stats
endpoints.freshness: Rolling timeframe window (default rolling; historical variant exists under v1beta1)

endpoints.name: TGM - Token OHLCV
optionalParams: token_address OR token_addresses (batch, max 5, mutually exclusive)
responseTopLevelFields: OHLCV candles: open/high/low/close, volume in token units and USD, market cap at each time point
endpoints.freshness: Unified OHLCV; recommended by docs over search prices which 'may be delayed'. Maximum historical depth not documented.
endpoints.redistribution: Allowed (token_ohlcv listed 'Allowed - None' in MCP guidelines)

endpoints.name: TGM - Nansen Indicators
responseTopLevelFields: risk_indicators (btc-reflexivity, liquidity-risk, concentration-risk, token-supply-inflation) and reward_indicators (chain-tvl, trading-range, price-momentum, chain-fees, protocol-fees, cex-flows, funding-rate); each with score (low/medium/high or bearish/neutral/bullish), signal, signal_percentile (0-100 vs same market-cap group), last_trigger_on
endpoints.freshness: Current state; historical point-in-time variant at /api/v1beta1/tgm/historical-token-quant-scores

endpoints.name: Search - General
endpoints.credits: 0 per MCP tool table (general_search is free)
responseTopLevelFields: tokens[] {name, symbol, chain, address, price, volume_24h, market_cap, rank}
entities[] {name, tags, rank}
total_results
endpoints.freshness: Docs warn: 'Prices may be delayed; use token OHLCV endpoints for real-time prices'

endpoints.name: Portfolio - DeFi Holdings
requiredParams: wallet address (per docs usage; exact schema field not extracted)
responseTopLevelFields: protocolsUsed[]
holdingsByProtocol (deposits/lendings, borrows, stakings, positions, locked, rewards, farms; each with type, summary, detail)
success
totalProtocols
endpoints.freshness: Active positions (current state); supported protocol list published at app.nansen.ai/portfolio/protocols
endpoints.redistribution: Allowed (address_portfolio 'Allowed - None'); note: excluded from x402 pay-per-call

endpoints.name: Prediction Market - Market Screener
responseTopLevelFields: Polymarket markets with volume, liquidity, price data
pagination
endpoints.freshness: Polymarket only. Related endpoints document freshness: orderbook polled from Polymarket CLOB API every ~60 seconds (MD5 change detection); OHLCV = 1-hour candles computed from on-chain OrderFilled events on Polygon.

endpoints.name: Backtesting - Historical Top Holders
endpoints.status: V1 Beta - subject to breaking changes
responseTopLevelFields: holders as of date with temporally-correct labels (resolved from label history to avoid forward-looking bias); value_usd priced at historical median price for as_of_date; no dust-filter default
endpoints.freshness: Point-in-time historical reconstruction; can be slow for high-volume tokens (USDC, native ETH). Snapshot endpoints take as_of_date; window endpoints take date_from + as_of_date. Historical endpoints cost 5x their real-time counterparts.

endpoints.name: Agent - Fast / Expert
responseTopLevelFields: Server-Sent Events stream (text/event-stream): delta {text}, tool_call {name}, finish {conversation_id, tool_calls[]}, error {error, status_code}; terminates with data: [DONE]
endpoints.freshness: Live agent answering over Nansen's on-chain data

assetClasses: Crypto on-chain analytics only: spot tokens (DEX/CEX flow analytics, balances, transfers), perpetual futures (Hyperliquid only), DeFi protocol positions, prediction markets (Polymarket only)
No equities, FX, fixed income, commodities, or traditional market data

chains.totalDocumented: 37 blockchain networks ('The Nansen API supports 37 blockchain networks across EVM and non-EVM ecosystems. Chain support varies by endpoint.')
chains.allKeyword: Some endpoints accept chains: ['all'], expanding to all chains supported by that endpoint
chains.perEndpointVariation: Smart Money chain enum has 19 chains (e.g. ethereum, solana, base, bnb, arbitrum, polygon, avalanche, optimism, linea, scroll, sei, sonic, plasma, monad, hyperevm, iotaevm, mantle, ronin + all); Smart Money 'Historical' support matrix lists only ethereum/solana/base/bnb; Token Screener enum has 27 chains; Bitcoin is Profiler-only (no Smart Money/TGM); TON has no PnL endpoints; Solana has no transaction lookup

coverage.markets: DEX trades across supported chains; CEX flows via labeled exchange wallets (not direct exchange market-data feeds); Hyperliquid for perps (token symbols, not addresses); Jupiter (Solana) for DCA data; Polymarket for prediction markets ('Nansen currently only supports data across Polymarket')
instrumentTypes: NFT transfers appear only in transaction-with-token-transfer-lookup
coverage.geographies: Not applicable / not documented (blockchain-native, no geographic segmentation)
coverage.labels: Proprietary wallet labeling: 'hundreds of millions of blockchain addresses' labeled (exchanges, funds, market makers, public figures); Smart Money = curated top 5,000 highest-performing wallets ranked by realized profit, winrate, performance across cycles; label taxonomy includes Fund, Smart Trader, 30D/90D/180D Smart Trader, Smart HL Perps Trader, plus extended labels (Public Figure, Exchange, Whale, BananaGun/Maestro Bot User)

freshness.general: FAQ: 'Data is refreshed at varying intervals, with many endpoints updating in real-time depending on the data type'; 'Typical data latency is low, often within seconds, depending on the chain being queried'
freshness.smartMoneyDexTrades: real-time, but window limited to last 24 hours
freshness.smartMoneyHistoricalHoldings: EOD UTC daily snapshots; processing starts 05:00 UTC, typically available 07:00 UTC; current day excluded
freshness.tokenScreener: near-real-time (to_date must be within 5 minutes of now); minute/hourly/daily granularity auto-selected
freshness.searchPrices: 'Prices may be delayed; use token OHLCV endpoints for real-time prices'
freshness.predictionMarketOrderbook: polled from Polymarket CLOB API every ~60 seconds with MD5 change detection
freshness.predictionMarketOhlcv: 1-hour candles from on-chain OrderFilled events on Polygon
freshness.pointsLeaderboard: tier data refreshes daily at 11:00 UTC
freshness.backtestingEndpoints: point-in-time historical reconstructions (not live); hash->timestamp resolution in historical transaction lookup can take 60-170 seconds without block_timestamp hint

historicalDepth.smartMoneyHistoricalHoldings: 4-year rolling window; earliest available date moves forward daily; max 4 years lookback on date filtering
historicalDepth.tokenScreener: retention only: minute ~130 minutes, hourly ~50 hours, daily ~50 days (hint block); schema descriptions say 2-hour/2-day/2-month retention per granularity. Historical snapshots not supported on the live endpoint
historicalDepth.backtestingFamily: 'Pass any past date' - point-in-time reconstruction with temporally-correct labels; label-history coverage limits: smart_trader labels available from 2020+; whale/public_figure/top_pnl/exchange segment coverage starts 2025-03-11; entity labels (historical transaction lookup) from 2025-03-11 onward; segment columns NULL where coverage doesn't reach date_to
historicalDepth.historicalAddressBalances_v1beta1: EVM (ethereum, base, bnb) and solana only; computed from on-chain transfer history up to as_of_date
historicalDepth.historicalAddressTransactions_v1beta1: capped at 20 most recent transfers as of snapshot date; ethereum, base, bnb, solana only
historicalDepth.dateFilterValidation: 'Maximum range is typically 1 year' for date filters (Filter Types reference)
historicalDepth.tokenOhlcv: maximum depth not documented (defaults to last 30 days if no date range given)

pagination.mechanism: Request body 'pagination': {page (1-based, default 1), per_page (default 10, max 1000)}. Response 'pagination': {page, per_page, is_last_page (boolean)}. No cursor tokens and no total-count field documented.
pagination.exceptions: profiler/address/transactions caps per_page at 100 (schema note; changelog mentions a 20/page performance cap in v1 migration); search/general uses 'limit' (max 50); search/entity-name has no pagination (capped 100 results); some endpoints return data only without pagination metadata; Points Leaderboard (GET) uses page + recordsPerPage (max 10,000)
pagination.bestPractice: Docs: always sort (order_by) when paginating for consistent ordering; add secondary sort to break ties

rateLimits.x402PerWallet: 5 requests/second, 60 requests/minute (separate from API-key limits); payments not charged for rate-limited requests
rateLimits.headers: Two layers: (1) RateLimit-Limit / RateLimit-Remaining / RateLimit-Reset plus X-RateLimit-Limit-Second / X-RateLimit-Remaining-Second / X-RateLimit-Limit-Minute / X-RateLimit-Remaining-Minute for overall limits; (2) bare X-RateLimit-Limit / X-RateLimit-Remaining / X-RateLimit-Reset for the most constrained app-enforced window. Credits headers: X-Nansen-Credits-Used, X-Nansen-Credits-Remaining.
rateLimits.backoffGuidance: 'Self-throttle using the smaller of RateLimit-Remaining and X-RateLimit-Remaining. If the API returns a 429 Too Many Requests response, honor Retry-After before retrying.' 429 body includes retry_after (seconds). No exponential-backoff algorithm is prescribed.
rateLimits.creditsQuotas: Credit-based metering on top of rate limits: Free = 100 one-time trial credits + 10/day refill; Pro = 2,000 starter credits/month grant (docs also say 'one time grant') + flexi-credit purchases; purchased credits expire 1 year from purchase. Per-call costs range 0 (search) to 750 (agent/expert); premium-label variants of tgm/holders, tgm/pnl-leaderboard, tgm/perp-pnl-leaderboard, tgm/perp-positions cost 150 vs 5; labels cost 100 (common) / 500 (premium); historical endpoints cost 5x real-time counterparts.

statusCodes.400: Bad Request - malformed request, invalid parameters or format
statusCodes.401: Unauthorized - authentication failed (missing, invalid, or expired token); body example {"error": {"code": "UNAUTHORIZED", "message": "Invalid or missing API key"}}
statusCodes.402: Payment Required (x402/MPP pay-per-call challenges; Payment-Required and/or WWW-Authenticate: Payment headers)
statusCodes.403: Forbidden - auth succeeded but no permission (wrong subscription tier, exceeded credit limit, or Free plan sending premium_labels=true)
statusCodes.404: Not Found - resource or endpoint does not exist
statusCodes.422: Unprocessable Content - validation error; body {detail: [{loc, msg, type}]} (FastAPI-style)
statusCodes.429: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded; body {detail, retry_after}; headers Retry-After, X-RateLimit-*
statusCodes.500: Internal Server Error - retry later, contact support if persistent
statusCodes.504: Gateway Timeout - retry after a short wait; check for heavy queries

errors.bodySchema: OpenAPI error responses use {"detail": string} for 400/401/403/404/500; {"detail": [{loc[], msg, type}]} for 422; {"detail": string, "retry_after": integer} for 429. The Authentication page also shows an {"error": {code, message}} shape for 401/403 - two shapes appear in official docs.
errors.cliErrorCodes: Nansen CLI machine-readable codes: UNAUTHORIZED, CREDITS_EXHAUSTED, RATE_LIMITED, INVALID_ADDRESS, UNSUPPORTED_FILTER

planTiers.Free: 100 one-time trial credits + 10 credits/day refill; can purchase credits; 'Access all API endpoints available in PRO'; lower rate limits (15 rps/300 rpm); CANNOT use premium_labels=true (403)
planTiers.Pro: $49/month (annual) or $69/month (monthly) per docs Credits & Pricing Guide; 2,000 starter credits; flexi-credit purchases (credits expire 1 year from purchase); 75 rps/1,500 rpm; premium labels allowed

entitlements.creditPrice: $0.001 per credit with bulk discounts (nansen.ai/api marketing page); the same page describes '100 Free credits' and '1,000 Pro credits' (differs from docs' 2,000 - see openQuestions)
entitlements.payPerCall: x402/MPP tiers: Basic $0.01/call (screeners, balances, transactions, PnL, DEX trades, flows), Premium $0.05/call (counterparties, holders, PnL leaderboards, perp leaderboard), Smart Money $0.05/call (all smart-money/*). Labels endpoints excluded - Pro subscription only. defi-holdings also listed as excluded from x402.
entitlements.premiumLabelGating: premium_labels=true on tgm/holders, tgm/pnl-leaderboard, tgm/perp-pnl-leaderboard, perp-leaderboard consumes 150 credits and returns dbt-sourced premium labels (Smart Money, Fund, etc.); false returns free-tier labels at 5 credits. Free plan users get 403 with premium_labels=true.
entitlements.enterprise: No enterprise/institutional API tier is documented on the pages reviewed; backtesting page says 'Reach out to your account contact for example notebooks, backtesting templates, and volume pricing.'

sdks.name: Official SDKs (language libraries)
sdks.status: None. FAQ: 'Does Nansen offer SDKs, libraries, or tools for integrating analytics into third-party applications like DEXs or wallets?' -> 'No'. Docs examples use raw HTTP (curl, Python httpx).

sdks.name: nansen-cli
sdks.status: Official command-line interface (source: github.com/nansen-ai/nansen-cli); structured JSON output; self-documenting via free 'nansen schema' command; auth via env var or interactive login; --fields and --stream options.

sdks.name: Nansen MCP server
sdks.status: Official hosted MCP server (https://mcp.nansen.ai/ra/mcp), 24 tools, JSON-RPC 2.0 over Streamable HTTP/SSE, NANSEN-API-KEY header; one-click Claude Desktop .dxt install; consumes the same API credits.

licensing.source: Data Redistribution Guidelines (docs.nansen.ai, 'Updated as of 18/11/2025') + MCP Data Redistribution Guidelines; both defer to Nansen Terms of Service (https://www.nansen.ai/legal/terms-of-services)
licensing.attributionRequirements: Display 'Powered by Nansen API' visibly in product UI, OR clickable link to nansen.ai near the data display (text, logo, or footer form). Attribution NOT required for personal or internal-only use.
licensing.restrictedDataConditions: Restricted endpoints require: (1) approval via Google form, (2) 'significant modification' - output not reverse-engineerable to Nansen classifications, meaningfully combined with >=1 other substantial independent data source, novel insights beyond Nansen's own product, (3) attribution. Explicitly prohibited: API endpoints serving Nansen smart money data with <7d delay, webhook/real-time smart-money alerts, smart-money trade-copying services, public smart-money leaderboards/rankings, competing smart-money products.
licensing.storageCaching: No explicit storage/caching time limits are documented in the reviewed guidelines. The only caching note found is a recommendation to cache the Points API locally. Rules apply to 'API responses, MCP tools, and derived data'. Internal analysis of prohibited-tier data is allowed; external sharing is not.
licensing.otherRules: Redistribution does not exempt from rate limits; don't misrepresent data / include appropriate disclaimers / don't make unwarranted guarantees; don't build competing products or repackage data to commoditize Nansen's insights.

limitations: Crypto-only provider: no coverage of traditional asset classes; cannot serve equities/FX/fixed-income functions of a multi-asset platform.

Chain support varies significantly by endpoint (37 chains overall, but Smart Money ~19-chain enum with historical support on only 4 per the support matrix; Bitcoin is Profiler-only; TON lacks PnL; Solana lacks transaction lookup; Hyperliquid is perps-only and keyed by token symbol, not address).

Token Screener is near-real-time only: to_date must be within 5 minutes of now; retention 130 min (minute) / 50 h (hourly) / 50 d (daily); historical screening requires the separate v1beta1 endpoint at 5x credits.
Smart Money DEX trades limited to a rolling last-24h window on the live endpoint.
The entire Backtesting family is V1 Beta: 'Request shapes, response fields, and pricing may change'; smart-money/historical-holdings is also flagged beta with possible data corrections/revisions.

Temporal label coverage gaps in historical data: whale/public_figure/top_pnl/exchange segments only from 2025-03-11; smart_trader from 2020+; segment columns NULL before coverage - a survivorship/coverage consideration for backtests.
Historical transaction lookup can take 60-170 seconds for unknown hashes unless block_timestamp is supplied; historical top holders 'can be slow for high-volume tokens'.

premium_labels default is changing (true -> false per changelog dated 31-03-2026 / hints saying 'From April 1'); clients must pin the parameter explicitly to avoid silent label-tier and credit-cost changes (5 vs 150 credits).
Deprecated parameters still in schemas ('date' vs 'timeframe', 'only_smart_money' vs 'trader_type', 'sort_by' vs 'order_by') - integration should target the recommended forms.
Search endpoint prices 'may be delayed' - docs direct real-time pricing to token-ohlcv.
Pagination has no total-count field; is_last_page only. Docs warn ordering may change between pages unless order_by is set explicitly.
Two different error-body shapes appear in official docs ({detail: ...} FastAPI style vs {error: {code, message}}); clients should handle both.
No official language SDKs (FAQ says 'No'); only CLI + MCP + raw REST.
Prediction markets = Polymarket only; perps = Hyperliquid only.
Per-endpoint rate-limit overrides can be severe (profiler/perp-trades: 5 requests/minute).
No documented SLA, uptime commitment, or formal deprecation-notice window on the reviewed pages.
Symbol mapping: primary keys are chain + token_address (contract), except Hyperliquid perps which use ticker symbols - cross-referencing spot and perp data requires a mapping layer.

Smart Money classification is proprietary and periodically re-derived (30D/90D/180D windows); labels change over time - live endpoints reflect current labels, only v1beta1 endpoints provide temporally-correct labels.
Docs marketing page (nansen.ai/api) and docs credit guide disagree on Pro starter credits (1,000 vs 2,000) - confirm at purchase.

primaryUseCases: PRIMARY: On-chain smart-money flow intelligence (netflows, holdings, DEX trades of labeled funds/smart traders) - this is Nansen's differentiated, proprietary dataset; note strict redistribution limits (internal research use is safest).
PRIMARY: Wallet/entity due diligence and forensics (Profiler: balances, transactions, counterparties, related wallets, labels) across 30+ chains.
PRIMARY: Token holder-base and flow analytics (Token God Mode: holders by entity type, exchange flows, who-bought-sold, PnL leaderboards).
PRIMARY: Point-in-time on-chain backtesting datasets with temporally-correct labels (v1beta1 family) - rare capability, but beta and 5x credit cost.
PRIMARY: Hyperliquid perp positioning/copytrading analytics and Polymarket prediction-market analytics.
fallbackUseCases: FALLBACK: Token OHLCV price/market-cap data (available and redistribution-friendly, but depth undocumented and crypto-only; a dedicated market-data provider is a more natural primary).
FALLBACK: DeFi portfolio positions (single endpoint, protocol list maintained on app site).
FALLBACK: General token/entity search and metadata resolution (search prices may be delayed).
notSuitable: Public-facing redistribution of smart-money holdings/dex-trades/labels/PnL-leaderboard data (prohibited) or lightly-transformed smart-money netflow displays (restricted; requires approval + significant modification).
Sub-minute exchange-grade market data feeds (screener granularity is minute-level; orderbook data exists only for Polymarket at ~60s polling).
hardConstraints: Never present search-endpoint prices as real-time (docs say they may be delayed; use token-ohlcv).
Never expose Prohibited-tier data (smart-money holdings/dex-trades/dcas/perp-trades, address labels, PnL leaderboards, perp leaderboard) in any customer-facing surface; internal analysis only.
Attribution ('Powered by Nansen API' or link to nansen.ai) is mandatory when displaying attribution-tier data in client-facing products.
Redistributing smart-money-derived data requires prior written approval, significant transformation, and >=7d delay for anything resembling raw redistribution.
Budget both rate limits (plan + per-endpoint overrides) AND credits; monitor X-Nansen-Credits-Remaining and honor Retry-After on 429.
Pin premium_labels explicitly on affected endpoints to avoid the documented default flip (and 5 -> 150 credit jump).
Treat all /api/v1beta1/ endpoints as unstable (breaking changes allowed) - isolate behind adapters and contract tests.
Historical smart-money holdings only reach back 4 years (rolling); temporal label coverage for non-smart-trader segments starts 2025-03-11 - flag backtests crossing these boundaries.

sourcesConsulted.whatItVerified: Official docs root; product positioning (proprietary labeling of 'hundreds of millions' of addresses, multi-chain coverage); section structure (Getting Started, API Reference, endpoint categories incl. Smart Money, Profiler, Token Screener, Portfolio, Points, Hyperliquid, Agent, Prediction Markets, MCP).
Authentication mechanism (apikey header), base URL https://api.nansen.ai/api/v1/, quick-start curl/Python examples, standard response envelope {data, pagination{page, per_page, is_last_page}}.

Plan rate limits (Free 15 rps/300 rpm; Pro 75 rps/1,500 rpm), per-endpoint overrides (token-sectors 60/min, tgm/perp-trades 60/min, profiler/perp-trades 5/min, web-search & web-fetch 15/min), rate-limit response headers, 429 + Retry-After guidance.
Documented status codes 400/401/402/403/404/422/429/500/504 with meanings and recommended actions; JSON response format.

Complete endpoint catalog with per-endpoint credit costs across Smart Money, Agent, Profiler, Token God Mode, Prediction Markets, Portfolio, and Backtesting categories; all endpoints POST with JSON bodies.
37-chain claim, EVM/non-EVM examples, smart money label taxonomy, filter type quick reference, order_by sort structure.

Full official docs text export (page 1): authentication details and 401/403 error bodies; AI Agent access; rate limit header layering and self-throttle guidance; Credits & Pricing Guide (Pro $49/mo annual / $69/mo monthly, 2,000 starter credits, Free 100 credits + 10/day refill, 1-year credit expiry, full per-endpoint credit table); x402 and MPP(Tempo) pay-per-call rails, tiers ($0.01/$0.05), per-wallet x402 rate limits (5 rps/60 rpm), excluded labels endpoints, .well-known/x402 discovery; supported chains tables (24 EVM + 12 non-EVM + hyperliquid perps-only) and per-endpoint chain matrices; filter/sorting reference incl. 1-year max date range; embedded OpenAPI 3.1 specs (securityScheme apiKey-in-header; PaginationRequest max 1000/page; token-screener timeframes and retention; smart-money netflow/holdings request+response schemas; search/general schema; credits and rate-limit response headers; error response schemas incl. 422 detail array and 429 retry_after); endpoint prose (SM dex-trades last-24h real-time; historical-holdings beta, 4-year lookback, daily snapshots; TGM holders/pnl-leaderboard premium_labels notices; token screener retention hint; OHLCV timeframes; flow intelligence segments; portfolio defi-holdings response fields; Hyperliquid symbol-keyed endpoints; Backtesting v1beta1 family incl. as_of_date/date_from conventions, 5x pricing, label-history coverage dates, performance caveats; Prediction Market = Polymarket only, orderbook ~60s polling, 1h OHLCV from Polygon events; Points API on app.nansen.ai, daily 11:00 UTC refresh; Smart Alerts /api/v1/smart-alert CRUD with Telegram/Slack/Discord/webhook channels; API Changelog: 29-08-2025 beta->v1 restructuring and endpoint renames, 31-03-2026 premium_labels flag + Free-plan 403 enforcement; Nansen CLI (github.com/nansen-ai/nansen-cli) and CLI error codes; MCP server endpoint/auth and 24-tool table with credit costs).

Full official docs text export (page 2): MCP Data Redistribution Guidelines and API Data Redistribution Guidelines (updated 18/11/2025) - per-endpoint Allowed/Attribution/Restricted/Prohibited matrix, attribution wording options, 'significant modification' definition, prohibited use cases incl. <7d-delay redistribution of smart money data, approval process, ToS link; Data Methodology & Technical Reference (inclusive [from,to] date ranges; historical holdings methodology with day-median prices and no look-ahead bias; EOD UTC snapshots; 05:00/07:00 UTC availability; 4-year rolling window); FAQ (no SDKs; real-time-ish freshness statements; latency 'often within seconds'); Quant Signals / BTC Drop Reflexivity methodology and response fields; Useful Links (pricing at app.nansen.ai/account/switch-plans).

Marketing/pricing page: $0.001 per API credit with bulk discounts; free tier described as '100 Free credits'; Pro described there as '1,000 Pro credits' (conflicts with docs' 2,000 starter credits); x402 pay-per-call tiers ($0.01 basic, $0.05 premium); REST/MCP/CLI access methods; no enterprise tier pricing published.

Fetched 2026-07-17 during adapter implementation: exact ProfilerAddressBalancesRequest fields (address, entity_name, chain enum of 28 values incl. 'all', hide_spam_token default true, filters {value_usd, price_usd, token_amount, token_symbol, token_address, token_name}, pagination, order_by) and exact ProfilerBalance response item fields (chain, address, token_address, token_symbol, token_name, token_amount, price_usd, value_usd). Response 'address' is empty when entity_name is used. No raw base-unit amount and no token-decimals field are returned.

openQuestions: Pro plan starter credits discrepancy: docs Credits & Pricing Guide says 2,000 credits ('one time grant' yet labeled 'credits/month'), while nansen.ai/api marketing page says 1,000 Pro credits - and whether the grant is monthly or one-time is ambiguous in the docs table itself. Needs confirmation with sales/dashboard.
No formal deprecation-notice window, SLA, or uptime commitment documented - required for institutional procurement; needs contract-level confirmation.
Exact freshness/latency SLOs per dataset are not quantified (only qualitative 'real-time'/'within seconds' statements); confirm before labeling any Nansen-sourced data as real-time in client UIs.

Data storage/caching duration limits are not addressed in the redistribution guidelines - the Terms of Service (nansen.ai/legal/terms-of-services) must be reviewed by counsel for storage, retention, and derived-works terms.
'/api/v1/search/web-search' and '/api/v1/search/web-fetch' appear in the rate-limits page but not in the endpoints overview or reference - scope and availability unclear.
Token OHLCV maximum historical depth is undocumented.

The '37 blockchains' headline vs the enumerated 24 EVM + 12 non-EVM (+ hyperliquid) tables and smaller per-endpoint enums - the effective chain list for any given workload must be validated per endpoint (e.g. via the CLI 'nansen schema' or endpoint enums).

Whether prohibited-tier data (e.g. smart-money holdings) may be shown to a firm's own investment team inside an internal research platform is 'internal use only - allowed', but showing it to external fund clients of a research platform likely counts as redistribution - needs legal confirmation for this platform's user base.
Premium labels default-flip timing (changelog says 31-03-2026 / 'April 1') vs current live behavior should be verified empirically at integration time.

Free-plan endpoint access: docs say Free can 'access all API endpoints available in PRO', but x402 page says labels require Pro subscription - whether Free-plan API keys can call /labels (at 100/500 credits) is not fully unambiguous.
